936	    ///
937	    /// There are three kinds from cache entries: Wheel metadata and policy as `MsgPack` files, the
938	    /// wheels themselves, and the unzipped wheel archives. If a wheel file is over an in-memory
939	    /// size threshold, we first download the zip file into the cache, then unzip it into a
940	    /// directory with the same name (exclusive of the `.whl` extension).
941	    ///
942	    /// Cache structure:
943	    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/pypi/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`
944	    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/<digest(index-url)>/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`
945	    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/url/<digest(url)>/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`
946	    ///
947	    /// See `uv_client::RegistryClient::wheel_metadata` for information on how wheel metadata
948	    /// is fetched.
949	    ///
950	    /// # Example
951	    ///
952	    /// Consider the following `requirements.in`:

958	    /// ```

1034	    /// Source distributions, wheels built from source distributions, their extracted metadata, and the
1035	    /// cache policy of the source distribution.
1036	    ///
1037	    /// The structure is similar of that of the `Wheel` bucket, except we have an additional layer
1038	    /// for the source distribution filename and the metadata is at the source distribution-level,
1039	    /// not at the wheel level.
1040	    ///
1041	    /// TODO(konstin): The cache policy should be on the source distribution level, the metadata we
1042	    /// can put next to the wheels as in the `Wheels` bucket.
1043	    ///
1044	    /// The unzipped source distribution is stored in a directory matching the source distribution
1045	    /// archive name.
1046	    ///
1047	    /// Source distributions are built into zipped wheel files (as PEP 517 specifies) and unzipped
1048	    /// lazily before installing. So when resolving, we only build the wheel and store the archive
1049	    /// file in the cache, when installing, we unpack it under the same name (exclusive of the
1050	    /// `.whl` extension). You may find a mix of wheel archive zip files and unzipped wheel
1051	    /// directories in the cache.
1052	    ///
1053	    /// Cache structure:
1054	    ///  * `built-wheels-v0/pypi/foo/34a17436ed1e9669/{manifest.msgpack, metadata.msgpack, foo-1.0.0.zip, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl, ...other wheels}`
1055	    ///  * `built-wheels-v0/<digest(index-url)>/foo/foo-1.0.0.zip/{manifest.msgpack, metadata.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl, ...other wheels}`
1056	    ///  * `built-wheels-v0/url/<digest(url)>/foo/foo-1.0.0.zip/{manifest.msgpack, metadata.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl, ...other wheels}`
1057	    ///  * `built-wheels-v0/git/<digest(url)>/<git sha>/foo/foo-1.0.0.zip/{metadata.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl, ...other wheels}`
1058	    ///
1059	    /// But the url filename does not need to be a valid source dist filename
1060	    /// (<https://github.com/search?q=path%3A**%2Frequirements.txt+master.zip&type=code>),
1061	    /// so it could also be the following and we have to take any string as filename:
1062	    ///  * `built-wheels-v0/url/<sha256(url)>/master.zip/metadata.msgpack`
1063	    ///
1064	    /// # Example
1065	    ///
1066	    /// The following requirements:
1067	    /// ```text
1068	    /// # git source dist
1069	    /// pydantic-extra-types @ git+https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic-extra-types.git
1070	    /// # pypi source dist
1071	    /// django_allauth==0.51.0
1072	    /// # url source dist
1073	    /// werkzeug @ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/0d/cc/ff1904eb5eb4b455e442834dabf9427331ac0fa02853bf83db817a7dd53d/werkzeug-3.0.1.tar.gz
1074	    /// ```
1075	    ///
1076	    /// ...may be cached as:
1077	    /// ```text
1078	    /// built-wheels-v4/
1079	    /// ├── git
1080	    /// │   └── 2122faf3e081fb7a
1081	    /// │       └── 7a2d650a4a7b4d04
1082	    /// │           ├── metadata.msgpack
1083	    /// │           └── pydantic_extra_types-2.9.0-py3-none-any.whl
1084	    /// ├── pypi
1085	    /// │   └── django-allauth
1086	    /// │       └── 0.51.0
1087	    /// │           ├── 0gH-_fwv8tdJ7JwwjJsUc
1088	    /// │           │   ├── django-allauth-0.51.0.tar.gz
1089	    /// │           │   │   └── [UNZIPPED CONTENTS]
1090	    /// │           │   ├── django_allauth-0.51.0-py3-none-any.whl
1091	    /// │           │   └── metadata.msgpack
1092	    /// │           └── revision.http
1093	    /// └── url
1094	    ///     └── 6781bd6440ae72c2
1095	    ///         ├── APYY01rbIfpAo_ij9sCY6
1096	    ///         │   ├── metadata.msgpack
1097	    ///         │   ├── werkzeug-3.0.1-py3-none-any.whl
1098	    ///         │   └── werkzeug-3.0.1.tar.gz
1099	    ///         │       └── [UNZIPPED CONTENTS]
1100	    ///         └── revision.http
1101	    /// ```
1102	    ///
1103	    /// Structurally, the `manifest.msgpack` is empty, and only contains the caching information
1104	    /// needed to invalidate the cache. The `metadata.msgpack` contains the metadata of the source
1105	    /// distribution.

1111	    ///

## UV Cache Architecture Report

I've thoroughly explored the uv codebase and mapped out the cache system structure. Here's what I found:

### 1. **Cache Buckets** 

The cache system uses versioned bucket directories defined in the `CacheBucket` enum. All buckets are located under the cache root, with each having a versioned directory name.

**Bucket variants and their versioned directories:**

| Bucket | Directory | Purpose |
|--------|-----------|---------|
| `Wheels` | `wheels-v6/` | Downloaded wheel files, metadata, and unzipped wheels |
| `SourceDistributions` | `sdists-v9/` | Source distributions, built wheels from sources, extracted metadata |
| `FlatIndex` | `flat-index-v2/` | Flat index API responses (cached as MsgPack) |
| `Git` | `git-v0/` | Git repository clones |
| `Interpreter` | `interpreter-v4/` | Python interpreter information at paths (MsgPack files) |
| `Simple` | `simple-v21/` | Simple index metadata API responses (rkyv format) |
| `Archive` | `archive-v0/` | Unzipped wheels stored as directories (internal use) |
| `Builds` | `builds-v0/` | Ephemeral virtual environments for PEP 517 builds |
| `Environments` | `environments-v2/` | Reusable virtual environments for tool execution |
| `Python` | `python-v0/` | Cached Python downloads |
| `Binaries` | `binaries-v0/` | Downloaded tool binaries (e.g., Ruff) |

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775495709334-80x2ex/crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs` (lines 934-1201)

### 2. **Cache Struct Definition**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775495709334-80x2ex/crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs` (lines 164-402)

```rust

    root: PathBuf,                              // The cache root directory

    temp_dir: Option<Arc<tempfile::TempDir>>,  // Temporary cache if --no-cache
    lock_file: Option<Arc<LockedFile>>,        // Exclusive lock for operations
}
```

**Key Cache methods:**

- `from_path(root)` - Create cache from explicit path
- `temp()` - Create temporary cache

### 3. **Size Calculation Implementation**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775495709334-80x2ex/crates/uv/src/commands/cache_size.rs`

The size calculation is straightforward and uses the `diskus` crate:

```rust
pub(crate) fn cache_size(
    cache: &Cache,
    human_readable: bool,
    printer: Printer,
    preview: Preview,
) -> Result<ExitStatus> {
    // Check if cache exists
    if !cache.root().exists() {
        return Ok(ExitStatus::Success);
    }

    // Use diskus crate to recursively calculate directory size
    let disk_usage = DiskUsage::new(vec![cache.root().to_path_buf()]);
    let total_bytes = disk_usage.count_ignoring_errors();

    // Output in human-readable or raw bytes
    if human_readable {
        let (bytes, unit) = human_readable_bytes(total_bytes);
        writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "{bytes:.1}{unit}")?;
    } else {
        writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "{total_bytes}")?;
    }

    Ok(ExitStatus::Success)
}
```

**Key points:**
- Uses `diskus::DiskUsage` crate for size calculation
- Recursively counts all bytes under `cache.root()`
- Ignores errors during traversal
- Converts to human-readable format when requested
- Feature-gated as experimental preview feature (`PreviewFeature::CacheSize`)

### 4. **Size and Directory Utilities**

**Removal statistics struct** (`removal.rs`, lines 44-55):

```rust
pub struct Removal {
    pub num_files: u64,        // Number of files removed
    pub num_dirs: u64,         // Number of directories removed
    pub total_bytes: u64,      // Total bytes removed (exact size, not block-aligned)
}
```

**Note:** The comment indicates this is "exact byte size (as opposed to block size)" and can over-count for hard-linked files.

**Directory recursion function:**

- `rm_rf(path)` - Recursive removal returning `Removal` stats
- Uses `walkdir::WalkDir` with `contents_first(true)` for depth-first traversal
- Tracks bytes via `metadata.len()` on each file
- Handles permission-denied errors gracefully

**Other directory utilities:**
- `directories(root)` - Lists subdirectories (from `uv_fs`)
- Operates on `CacheShard` and `CacheEntry` types for fine-grained path management

### 5. **Related Structures**

**WheelCache enum** (wheel.rs, lines 9-23):
```rust
pub enum WheelCache<'a> {
    Index(&'a IndexUrl),      // PyPI or alternative index
    Url(&'a DisplaySafeUrl),  // Direct URL dependency
    Path(&'a DisplaySafeUrl), // Path dependency
    Editable(&'a DisplaySafeUrl), // Editable dependency
    Git(&'a DisplaySafeUrl, &'a str), // Git dependency (URL + SHA)
}
```

**WheelCacheKind enum** (wheel.rs, lines 56-69):
- `Pypi` - PyPI data
- `Index` - Alternative indices
- `Url` - Direct URLs
- `Path` - Local paths
- `Editable` - Editable installs
- `Git` - Git repositories

**ArchiveId struct** (archive.rs):
```rust
pub struct ArchiveId(String);  // Unique identifier using nanoid
```

### 6. **Cache Directory Structure Example**

For wheels, the structure follows patterns like:
```
cache-root/
├── wheels-v6/
│   ├── pypi/
│   │   └── package-name/
│   │       └── package-name-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack
│   ├── index/
│   │   └── <digest(index-url)>/
│   │       └── package-name/
│   │           └── metadata.msgpack
│   └── url/
│       └── <digest(url)>/
│           └── package-name/
│               └── metadata.msgpack
├── archive-v0/
│   └── <archive-id>/
│       └── extracted-contents/
└── ... (other buckets)
```

### 7. **Command Integration Points**

- **`cache_size`** - Calculates total cache size using `diskus`
- **`cache_clean`** - Removes cache entries by package name, returns `Removal` stats
- **`cache_prune`** - Removes unreachable objects, tracks `Removal` stats
- **`cache_dir`** - Shows cache directory path

All commands use the `Cache::bucket()` method to access specific bucket directories and operate on them.

# Plan: `uv cache stats` subcommand

## Context

`uv cache size` (a preview feature, `1 << 11`) shows only one number – the total disk usage of
the entire cache root.  Users want a per-bucket breakdown so they can see how much space is used
by wheels, sdists, git repos, Python downloads, etc.  This plan adds a new `uv cache stats`
subcommand that prints one line per bucket with its size and a `Total` footer, supporting the same
`--human` / `-H` flag as `cache size`.

---

## Buckets and human-friendly labels

`CacheBucket` (in `crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs:934`) has 11 variants.  Their directory names
(from `to_str()`) and the proposed display labels are:

| Variant              | Directory       | Display label         |
|----------------------|-----------------|-----------------------|
| `Wheels`             | `wheels-v6`     | `Wheels`              |
| `SourceDistributions`| `sdists-v9`     | `Source distributions`|
| `FlatIndex`          | `flat-index-v2` | `Flat index`          |
| `Git`                | `git-v0`        | `Git`                 |
| `Interpreter`        | `interpreter-v4`| `Interpreter`         |
| `Simple`             | `simple-v21`    | `Simple index`        |
| `Archive`            | `archive-v0`    | `Archives`            |
| `Builds`             | `builds-v0`     | `Builds`              |
| `Environments`       | `environments-v2`| `Environments`       |
| `Python`             | `python-v0`     | `Python`              |
| `Binaries`           | `binaries-v0`   | `Binaries`            |

Note: `CacheBucket::iter()` (line 1316) intentionally excludes `Python` (it's not pruned/cleaned
the same way).  The `cache stats` command should still report it, so the bucket list in
`cache_stats.rs` is defined independently.

---

## Step-by-step implementation

### Step 1 – Add `CacheBucket::name()` in `crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs`

After `to_str()` (around line 1202), add:

```rust
/// Return the human-readable display name for this cache bucket.
pub fn name(self) -> &'static str {
    match self {
        Self::Wheels              => "Wheels",
        Self::SourceDistributions => "Source distributions",
        Self::FlatIndex           => "Flat index",
        Self::Git                 => "Git",
        Self::Interpreter         => "Interpreter",
        Self::Simple              => "Simple index",
        Self::Archive             => "Archives",
        Self::Builds              => "Builds",
        Self::Environments        => "Environments",
        Self::Python              => "Python",
        Self::Binaries            => "Binaries",
    }
}
```

---

### Step 2 – Add `CacheStats` preview feature in `crates/uv-preview/src/lib.rs`

**a)** In the `PreviewFeature` enum (around line 196), append after `ProjectDirectoryMustExist`:

```rust
CacheStats = 1 << 28,
```

**b)** In `as_str()` (around line 231), add:

```rust
Self::CacheStats => "cache-stats",
```

**c)** In `FromStr` impl (around line 277), add:

```rust
"cache-stats" => Self::CacheStats,
```

**d)** In the unit test `test_preview_feature_as_str` (around line 525), add:

```rust
assert_eq!(PreviewFeature::CacheStats.as_str(), "cache-stats");
```

---

### Step 3 – Add `Stats(StatsArgs)` to the CLI in `crates/uv-cli/src/lib.rs`

**a)** In `CacheCommand` enum (line 878), append after `Size(SizeArgs)` (line 904):

```rust
/// Show a per-bucket breakdown of cache usage.
///
/// Displays the disk usage of each cache bucket (wheels, source distributions, Git
/// repositories, etc.) with a total at the end. By default outputs raw bytes; use
/// `--human` for human-readable output.
Stats(StatsArgs),
```

**b)** After the `SizeArgs` struct (around line 952), add:

```rust
#[derive(Args, Debug)]
pub struct StatsArgs {
    /// Display sizes in human-readable format (e.g., `1.2 GiB` instead of raw bytes).
    #[arg(long = "human", short = 'H', alias = "human-readable")]
    pub human: bool,
}
```

---

### Step 4 – Create `crates/uv/src/commands/cache_stats.rs`

New file. Pattern is identical to `cache_size.rs` with the per-bucket loop added.

```rust
use std::fmt::Write;

use anyhow::Result;
use diskus::DiskUsage;

use crate::commands::{ExitStatus, human_readable_bytes};
use crate::printer::Printer;
use uv_cache::{Cache, CacheBucket};
use uv_preview::{Preview, PreviewFeature};
use uv_warnings::warn_user;

/// Display a per-bucket breakdown of cache usage.
pub(crate) fn cache_stats(
    cache: &Cache,
    human_readable: bool,
    printer: Printer,
    preview: Preview,
) -> Result<ExitStatus> {
    if !preview.is_enabled(PreviewFeature::CacheStats) {
        warn_user!(
            "`uv cache stats` is experimental and may change without warning. \
             Pass `--preview-features {}` to disable this warning.",
            PreviewFeature::CacheStats
        );
    }

    // All buckets to report, including Python (excluded from CacheBucket::iter()
    // because pruning/clean don't touch Python downloads, but we still want to
    // show it in the stats breakdown).

    let sep_width = col + 2 + if human_readable { 12 } else { 20 };
    writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "{}", "-".repeat(sep_width))?;

    writeln!(
        printer.stdout_important(),
        "{:<col$}  {}",
        "Total",
        fmt_size(total_bytes)
    )?;

    Ok(ExitStatus::Success)
}
```

use std::fmt::Write;

use anyhow::Result;
use diskus::DiskUsage;

use crate::commands::{ExitStatus, human_readable_bytes};
use crate::printer::Printer;
use uv_cache::{Cache, CacheBucket};
use uv_preview::{Preview, PreviewFeature};
use uv_warnings::warn_user;

/// Display a per-bucket breakdown of cache usage.
pub(crate) fn cache_stats(
    cache: &Cache,
    human_readable: bool,
    printer: Printer,
    preview: Preview,
) -> Result<ExitStatus> {
    if !preview.is_enabled(PreviewFeature::CacheStats) {
        warn_user!(
            "`uv cache stats` is experimental and may change without warning. \
             Pass `--preview-features {}` to disable this warning.",
            PreviewFeature::CacheStats
        );
    }

    // All buckets to report, including Python (excluded from CacheBucket::iter()
    // because pruning/clean don't touch Python downloads, but we still want to
    // show it in the stats breakdown).
    let buckets = [
        CacheBucket::Wheels,
        CacheBucket::SourceDistributions,
        CacheBucket::FlatIndex,
        CacheBucket::Git,
        CacheBucket::Interpreter,
        CacheBucket::Simple,
        CacheBucket::Archive,
        CacheBucket::Builds,
        CacheBucket::Environments,
        CacheBucket::Python,
        CacheBucket::Binaries,
    ];

    let mut rows: Vec<(&'static str, u64)> = Vec::with_capacity(buckets.len());
    let mut total_bytes: u64 = 0;

    for bucket in buckets {
        let path = cache.bucket(bucket);
        let size = if path.exists() {
            DiskUsage::new(vec![path]).count_ignoring_errors()
        } else {
            0
        };
        total_bytes = total_bytes.saturating_add(size);
        rows.push((bucket.name(), size));
    }

    // Column width: widest label, minimum covers "Total".
    let col = rows
        .iter()
        .map(|(label, _)| label.len())
        .max()
        .unwrap_or(0)
        .max("Total".len());

    // Format size value.
    let fmt_size = |bytes: u64| -> String {
        if human_readable {
            let (val, unit) = human_readable_bytes(bytes);
            format!("{val:.1}{unit}")
        } else {
            bytes.to_string()
        }
    };

    for (label, size) in &rows {
        writeln!(
            printer.stdout_important(),
            "{label:<col$}  {}",
            fmt_size(*size)
        )?;
    }

    // Separator – dashes spanning the label column plus a fixed size column.
    let sep_width = col + 2 + if human_readable { 12 } else { 20 };
    writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "{}", "-".repeat(sep_width))?;

    writeln!(
        printer.stdout_important(),
        "{:<col$}  {}",
        "Total",
        fmt_size(total_bytes)
    )?;

    Ok(ExitStatus::Success)
}
```

# Plan: `uv cache stats` subcommand

## Context

`uv cache size` (a preview feature, `1 << 11`) shows only one number – the total disk usage of
the entire cache root. Users want a per-bucket breakdown so they can see how much space is used
by wheels, sdists, git repos, Python downloads, etc. This plan adds a new `uv cache stats`
subcommand that prints one line per bucket with its size and a `Total` footer, supporting the same
`--human` / `-H` flag as `cache size`.

---

## Buckets and human-friendly labels

`CacheBucket` (in `crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs:934`) has 11 variants. Their directory names
(from `to_str()`) and the proposed display labels are:

| Variant               | Directory         | Display label          |
| --------------------- | ----------------- | ---------------------- |
| `Wheels`              | `wheels-v6`       | `Wheels`               |
| `SourceDistributions` | `sdists-v9`       | `Source distributions` |
| `FlatIndex`           | `flat-index-v2`   | `Flat index`           |
| `Git`                 | `git-v0`          | `Git`                  |
| `Interpreter`         | `interpreter-v4`  | `Interpreter`          |
| `Simple`              | `simple-v21`      | `Simple index`         |
| `Archive`             | `archive-v0`      | `Archives`             |
| `Builds`              | `builds-v0`       | `Builds`               |
| `Environments`        | `environments-v2` | `Environments`         |
| `Python`              | `python-v0`       | `Python`               |
| `Binaries`            | `binaries-v0`     | `Binaries`             |

use assert_cmd::assert::OutputAssertExt;

use uv_test::uv_snapshot;

/// Test that `cache stats` shows all-zero rows for an empty cache (raw output).
#[test]
fn cache_stats_empty_raw() {
    let context = uv_test::test_context!("3.12");
